gazette: The Directorate General of International Agreements and the European Unio
title: A notice has been published stating that it is not possible to use the REX number for exports to third countries, including the UK and South Korea
bodyHtml:
A notice has been published stating that it is not possible to use the REX number for exports to third countries, including the UK and South Korea.

The Directorate General of International Agreements and the European Union has issued a notice stating that it is not possible to use the REX number for exports to third countries, including the UK and South Korea.

The notice states:

  • It has been understood that companies apply to the Regional Directorates of Customs and Foreign Trade to obtain a registered exporter (REX) number for exports to countries such as the UK and South Korea.
  • The application area of the REX system is quite limited for exporters located in our country; there is no possibility or obligation to use the REX number for exports from Turkey to third countries, including the UK and South Korea.
  • The REX origin declaration can only be issued as specified in Article 5, paragraph 3(a) of Decision No. 2018/501, for products originating from beneficiary countries listed in Annex-I of the current Import Regime Decision, with the aim of sending them to the European Union and Switzerland.

Please click for the related notice.
